title: Classification of Discrete-Time Systems
summary: The page concludes a discussion of advantages and disadvantages of digital systems, noting benefits such as multiplexing and reliable reproduction along with drawbacks like interface complexity and power consumption. It then begins subsection 3.4-1 on classification of discrete-time systems, introducing linearity and time invariance and showing with the example y[n] = e^{-n}x[n] that a system can be time-varying.
